artPrompt: A tense midway moment: a glowing six-sided die tumbling through firelit air above an upturned card, a gambler's anxious hand frozen mid-bet, ember sparks drifting through carnival-red haze, rigged wheels and lurid neon dissolving into smoky black behind, the cruel all-or-nothing instant suspended in hot-gold light, tender beneath the menace, rendered as soft pastel impressionism — hazy diffuse light, visible chalky texture, gentle gradients, dreamy muted color.
name: Double Down or Double Over
text:
Look at the top card of your library and bet X life that it costs more than the value of a die you're about to roll. Roll a six-sided die. If the card's mana value is greater than the result, double your X: draw cards equal to X and create X Treasures. If it's not, double your over: lose X life, then lose X life again, then sit down.
If X was 0, nothing happens and the table sighs at you.
flavorText: There is no middle outcome. There has never been a middle outcome. That's why it's fun.

extras.dice: bet life on a card vs. a die roll
